learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an advanced level of understanding of who the customer is in health care, different customer value propositions in health care and ways to facilitate strong value-based relationships with various customers.
Understand to an advanced level the different systems involved in running a business (public or private).
Demonstrate a comprehensive understanding of the professional and ethical responsibilities in accordance with the Australian legal and regulatory requirements.

assessments.task_description:
NDIS planning and reflective assessment task to be completed at the end of the private practice and NDIS modules.

This is a MUST PASS component of the course.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The NDIS Planning and Reflection Assignment is to be submitted via the PHTY7889 Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assignment involves writing a proposal or grant application for a new physiotherapy service. Students will apply what they have learned from module 1 and module 2 to complete this proposal.

Select one (1) specific program or service offering that you have had experience within, had exposure to, or identified as a potential opportunity for service improvement or development, in one of your clinical immersions. If you have not experienced any clinical immersions to date – you can use your personal experience of visits to any healthcare setting. Your service offering or program should be set in the private healthcare system and within a small to medium private physiotherapy clinic.

Imagine that your employer has asked you to assist in the writing of a proposal or grant application to propose a new physiotherapy service idea. The report will focus on i) the Customer Profile and Value Proposition, ii) the Marketing Plan, iii) the Cost/Benefit to your organisation, and iv) Written Communication and originality. You must demonstrate that this service or program will generate benefits for your employer

The maximum submission length is 4 pages, not including references (only 2000 words will be marked as per Assessment Procedure (86)). 

This is a MUST PASS component of the course.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The Physiotherapy Service Proposal Assignment is to be submitted via the PHTY7889 Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This in-person online 120 minutes (+10 minutes planning time) written theory examination comprises two parts:

  • Part A will examine the ability to discuss the physiotherapy management of a complex case randomly allocated from one of three (3) sub-discipline areas of clinical practice (one (1) of the three cases provided in the exam will be randomly allocated to the student, to which they respond). Responses for the complex case that you are allocated will need to address aspects of appropriate assessment, the findings relevant to the case, and evidence-based management planning.
  • Part B will examine the ability to identify and manage ethical and professional considerations in physiotherapy practice. 


The exam will be completed in person on campus, via Inspera.

This is a MUST PASS component of the course.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The End of Semester Theory Examination will be submitted automatically within the Inspera platform .
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Full criteria for each grade is available in the Assessment Procedure.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 6(27 Jul - 06 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Professional Practice Environment

Students will have access to a series of online eLearning modules and online presentations (webinars). Topics will include professional practice, working with compensable bodies, and customer service.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
